37	// AdmissionOptions holds the admission options.
38	// It is a wrap of generic AdmissionOptions.
39	type AdmissionOptions struct {
40		// GenericAdmission holds the generic admission options.
41		GenericAdmission *genericoptions.AdmissionOptions
42		// DEPRECATED flag, should use EnabledAdmissionPlugins and DisabledAdmissionPlugins.
43		// They are mutually exclusive, specify both will lead to an error.
44		PluginNames []string
45	}
46	
47	// NewAdmissionOptions creates a new instance of AdmissionOptions
48	// Note:
49	//
50	//	In addition it calls RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins to register
51	//	all kube-apiserver admission plugins.
52	//
53	//	Provides the list of RecommendedPluginOrder that holds sane values
54	//	that can be used by servers that don't care about admission chain.
55	//	Servers that do care can overwrite/append that field after creation.
56	func NewAdmissionOptions() *AdmissionOptions {
57		options := genericoptions.NewAdmissionOptions()
58		// register all admission plugins
59		RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins(options.Plugins)
60		// set RecommendedPluginOrder
61		options.RecommendedPluginOrder = AllOrderedPlugins
62		// set DefaultOffPlugins
63		options.DefaultOffPlugins = DefaultOffAdmissionPlugins()
64	
65		return &AdmissionOptions{
66			GenericAdmission: options,
67		}
68	}
69	
70	// AddFlags adds flags related to admission for kube-apiserver to the specified FlagSet
71	func (a *AdmissionOptions) AddFlags(fs *pflag.FlagSet) {
72		if a == nil {
73			return
74		}
75		registerAllAdmissionPluginFlags(fs)
76		fs.StringSliceVar(&a.PluginNames, "admission-control", a.PluginNames, ""+
77			"Admission is divided into two phases. "+
78			"In the first phase, only mutating admission plugins run. "+
79			"In the second phase, only validating admission plugins run. "+
80			"The names in the below list may represent a validating plugin, a mutating plugin, or both. "+
81			"The order of plugins in which they are passed to this flag does not matter. "+
82			"Comma-delimited list of: "+strings.Join(a.GenericAdmission.Plugins.Registered(), ", ")+".")
83		fs.MarkDeprecated("admission-control", "Use --enable-admission-plugins or --disable-admission-plugins instead. Will be removed in a future version.")
84		fs.Lookup("admission-control").Hidden = false
85	
86		a.GenericAdmission.AddFlags(fs)
87	}
88	
89	// Validate verifies flags passed to kube-apiserver AdmissionOptions.
90	// Kube-apiserver verifies PluginNames and then call generic AdmissionOptions.Validate.
91	func (a *AdmissionOptions) Validate() []error {
92		if a == nil {
93			return nil
94		}
95		var errs []error
96		if a.PluginNames != nil &&
97			(a.GenericAdmission.EnablePlugins != nil || a.GenericAdmission.DisablePlugins != nil) {
98			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("admission-control and enable-admission-plugins/disable-admission-plugins flags are mutually exclusive"))
99		}
100	
101		registeredPlugins := sets.NewString(a.GenericAdmission.Plugins.Registered()...)
102		for _, name := range a.PluginNames {
103			if !registeredPlugins.Has(name) {
104				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("admission-control plugin %q is unknown", name))
105			}
106		}
107	
108		errs = append(errs, a.GenericAdmission.Validate()...)
109	
110		return errs
111	}
112	
113	// ApplyTo adds the admission chain to the server configuration.
114	// Kube-apiserver just call generic AdmissionOptions.ApplyTo.
115	func (a *AdmissionOptions) ApplyTo(
116		c *server.Config,
117		informers informers.SharedInformerFactory,
118		kubeClient kubernetes.Interface,
119		dynamicClient dynamic.Interface,
120		features featuregate.FeatureGate,
121		effectiveVersion compatibility.EffectiveVersion,
122		pluginInitializers ...admission.PluginInitializer,
123	) error {
124		if a == nil {
125			return nil
126		}
127	
128		if a.PluginNames != nil {
129			// pass PluginNames to generic AdmissionOptions
130			a.GenericAdmission.EnablePlugins, a.GenericAdmission.DisablePlugins = computePluginNames(a.PluginNames, a.GenericAdmission.RecommendedPluginOrder)
131		}
132	
133		return a.GenericAdmission.ApplyTo(c, informers, kubeClient, dynamicClient, features, effectiveVersion, pluginInitializers...)
134	}
135	
136	// explicitly disable all plugins that are not in the enabled list
137	func computePluginNames(explicitlyEnabled []string, all []string) (enabled []string, disabled []string) {
138		return explicitlyEnabled, sets.NewString(all...).Difference(sets.NewString(explicitlyEnabled...)).List()
139	}

122	// ApplyTo adds the admission chain to the server configuration.
123	// In case admission plugin names were not provided by a cluster-admin they will be prepared from the recommended/default values.
124	// In addition the method lazily initializes a generic plugin that is appended to the list of pluginInitializers
125	// note this method uses:
126	//
127	//	genericconfig.Authorizer
128	func (a *AdmissionOptions) ApplyTo(
129		c *server.Config,
130		informers informers.SharedInformerFactory,
131		kubeClient kubernetes.Interface,
132		dynamicClient dynamic.Interface,
133		features featuregate.FeatureGate,
134		effectiveVersion compatibility.EffectiveVersion,
135		pluginInitializers ...admission.PluginInitializer,
136	) error {
137		if a == nil {
138			return nil
139		}
140	
141		// Admission depends on CoreAPI to set SharedInformerFactory and ClientConfig.
142		if informers == nil {
143			return fmt.Errorf("admission depends on a Kubernetes core API shared informer, it cannot be nil")
144		}
145		if kubeClient == nil || dynamicClient == nil {
146			return fmt.Errorf("admission depends on a Kubernetes core API client, it cannot be nil")
147		}
148	
149		pluginNames := a.enabledPluginNames()
150	
151		pluginsConfigProvider, err := admission.ReadAdmissionConfiguration(pluginNames, a.ConfigFile, configScheme)
152		if err != nil {
153			return fmt.Errorf("failed to read plugin config: %v", err)
154		}
155	
156		discoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(kubeClient.Discovery())
157		discoveryRESTMapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(discoveryClient)
158		genericInitializer := initializer.New(kubeClient, dynamicClient, informers, c.Authorization.Authorizer, features,
159			effectiveVersion, c.DrainedNotify(), discoveryRESTMapper)
160		initializersChain := admission.PluginInitializers{initializer.NewAPIServerIDInitializer(c.APIServerID), genericInitializer}
161		initializersChain = append(initializersChain, pluginInitializers...)
162	
163		admissionPostStartHook := func(hookContext server.PostStartHookContext) error {
164			discoveryRESTMapper.Reset()
165			go utilwait.Until(discoveryRESTMapper.Reset, 30*time.Second, hookContext.Done())
166			return nil
167		}
168	
169		err = c.AddPostStartHook("start-apiserver-admission-initializer", admissionPostStartHook)
170		if err != nil {
171			return fmt.Errorf("failed to add post start hook for policy admission: %w", err)
172		}
173	
174		admissionChain, err := a.Plugins.NewFromPlugins(pluginNames, pluginsConfigProvider, initializersChain, a.Decorators)
175		if err != nil {
176			return err
177		}
178	
179		c.AdmissionControl = admissionmetrics.WithStepMetrics(admissionChain)
180		return nil
181	}

183	// Validate verifies flags passed to AdmissionOptions.
184	func (a *AdmissionOptions) Validate() []error {
185		if a == nil {
186			return nil
187		}
188	
189		errs := []error{}
190	
191		registeredPlugins := sets.NewString(a.Plugins.Registered()...)
192		for _, name := range a.EnablePlugins {
193			if !registeredPlugins.Has(name) {
194				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("enable-admission-plugins plugin %q is unknown", name))
195			}
196		}
197	
198		for _, name := range a.DisablePlugins {
199			if !registeredPlugins.Has(name) {
200				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("disable-admission-plugins plugin %q is unknown", name))
201			}
202		}
203	
204		enablePlugins := sets.NewString(a.EnablePlugins...)
205		disablePlugins := sets.NewString(a.DisablePlugins...)
206		if len(enablePlugins.Intersection(disablePlugins).List()) > 0 {
207			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("%v in enable-admission-plugins and disable-admission-plugins "+
208				"overlapped", enablePlugins.Intersection(disablePlugins).List()))
209		}
210	
211		// Verify RecommendedPluginOrder.
212		recommendPlugins := sets.NewString(a.RecommendedPluginOrder...)
213		intersections := registeredPlugins.Intersection(recommendPlugins)
214		if !intersections.Equal(recommendPlugins) {
215			// Developer error, this should never run in.
216			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("plugins %v in RecommendedPluginOrder are not registered",
217				recommendPlugins.Difference(intersections).List()))
218		}
219		if !intersections.Equal(registeredPlugins) {
220			// Developer error, this should never run in.
221			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("plugins %v registered are not in RecommendedPluginOrder",
222				registeredPlugins.Difference(intersections).List()))
223		}
224	
225		return errs
226	}

228	// enabledPluginNames makes use of RecommendedPluginOrder, DefaultOffPlugins,
229	// EnablePlugins, DisablePlugins fields
230	// to prepare a list of ordered plugin names that are enabled.
231	func (a *AdmissionOptions) enabledPluginNames() []string {
232		allOffPlugins := append(sets.List[string](a.DefaultOffPlugins), a.DisablePlugins...)
233		disabledPlugins := sets.NewString(allOffPlugins...)
234		enabledPlugins := sets.NewString(a.EnablePlugins...)
235		disabledPlugins = disabledPlugins.Difference(enabledPlugins)
236	
237		orderedPlugins := []string{}
238		for _, plugin := range a.RecommendedPluginOrder {
239			if !disabledPlugins.Has(plugin) {
240				orderedPlugins = append(orderedPlugins, plugin)
241			}
242		}
243	
244		return orderedPlugins
245	}
246	
247	// Return names of plugins which are enabled by default
248	func (a *AdmissionOptions) defaultEnabledPluginNames() []string {
249		defaultOnPluginNames := []string{}
250		for _, pluginName := range a.RecommendedPluginOrder {
251			if !a.DefaultOffPlugins.Has(pluginName) {
252				defaultOnPluginNames = append(defaultOnPluginNames, pluginName)
253			}
254		}
255	
256		return defaultOnPluginNames
257	}

29	// Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request
30	// that is used to make an admission decision.
31	type Attributes interface {
32		// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client
33		// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return
34		// the empty string
35		GetName() string
36		// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)
37		GetNamespace() string
38		// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods
39		GetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource
40		// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.
41		// For instance, /pods has the resource "pods" and the kind "Pod", while /pods/foo/status has the resource "pods", the sub resource "status", and the kind "Pod"
42		// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource "pods", subresource "binding", and kind "Binding".
43		GetSubresource() string
44		// GetOperation is the operation being performed
45		GetOperation() Operation
46		// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed
47		GetOperationOptions() runtime.Object
48		// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent
49		// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.
50		// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it
51		// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.
52		IsDryRun() bool
53		// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied
54		GetObject() runtime.Object
55		// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.
56		GetOldObject() runtime.Object
57		// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod
58		GetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind
59		// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user
60		GetUserInfo() user.Info
61	
62		// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where
63		// "podsecuritypolicy" is the name of the plugin, "admission.k8s.io" is the name of the organization, "admit-policy" is the key name.
64		// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.
65		// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.
66		// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or
67		// equal to Metadata.

53	func createHandler(r rest.NamedCreater, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, includeName bool) http.HandlerFunc {
54		return func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
55			ctx := req.Context()
56			// For performance tracking purposes.
57			ctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, "Create", traceFields(req)...)
58			req = req.WithContext(ctx)
59			defer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)
60	
61			namespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)
62			if err != nil {
63				if includeName {
64					// name was required, return
65					scope.err(err, w, req)
66					return
67				}
68	
69				// otherwise attempt to look up the namespace
70				namespace, err = scope.Namer.Namespace(req)
71				if err != nil {
72					scope.err(err, w, req)
73					return
74				}
75			}
76	
77			// enforce a timeout of at most requestTimeoutUpperBound (34s) or less if the user-provided
78			// timeout inside the parent context is lower than requestTimeoutUpperBound.
79			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeoutUpperBound)
80			defer cancel()
81			outputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)
82			if err != nil {
83				scope.err(err, w, req)
84				return
85			}
86	
87			gv := scope.Kind.GroupVersion()
88			s, err := negotiation.NegotiateInputSerializer(req, false, scope.Serializer)
89			if err != nil {
90				scope.err(err, w, req)
91				return
92			}
93	
94			body, err := limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric(ctx, req, scope.MaxRequestBodyBytes, scope.Resource.GroupResource(), requestmetrics.Create)
95			if err != nil {
96				span.AddEvent("limitedReadBody failed", attribute.Int("len", len(body)), attribute.String("err", err.Error()))
97				scope.err(err, w, req)
98				return
99			}
100			span.AddEvent("limitedReadBody succeeded", attribute.Int("len", len(body)))
101	
102			options := &metav1.CreateOptions{}
103			values := req.URL.Query()
104			if err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(values, scope.MetaGroupVersion, options); err != nil {
105				err = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())
106				scope.err(err, w, req)
107				return
108			}
109			if errs := validation.ValidateCreateOptions(options); len(errs) > 0 {
110				err := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: "CreateOptions"}, "", errs)
111				scope.err(err, w, req)
112				return
113			}
114			options.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("CreateOptions"))
115	
116			defaultGVK := scope.Kind
117			original := r.New()
118	
119			validationDirective := fieldValidation(options.FieldValidation)
120			decodeSerializer := s.Serializer
121			if validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {
122				decodeSerializer = s.StrictSerializer
123			}
124	
125			decoder := scope.Serializer.DecoderToVersion(decodeSerializer, scope.HubGroupVersion)
126			span.AddEvent("About to convert to expected version")
127			obj, gvk, err := decoder.Decode(body, &defaultGVK, original)
128			if err != nil {
129				strictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)
130				switch {
131				case isStrictError && obj != nil && validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:
132					addStrictDecodingWarnings(req.Context(), strictError.Errors())
133				case isStrictError && validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationIgnore:
134					klog.Warningf("unexpected strict error when field validation is set to ignore")
135					fallthrough
136				default:
137					err = transformDecodeError(scope.Typer, err, original, gvk, body)
138					scope.err(err, w, req)
139					return
140				}
141			}
142	
143			objGV := gvk.GroupVersion()
144			if !scope.AcceptsGroupVersion(objGV) {
145				err = errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf("the API version in the data (%s) does not match the expected API version (%v)", objGV.String(), gv.String()))
146				scope.err(err, w, req)
147				return
148			}
149			span.AddEvent("Conversion done")
150	
151			// On create, get name from new object if unset
152			if len(name) == 0 {
153				_, name, _ = scope.Namer.ObjectName(obj)
154			}
155			if len(namespace) == 0 && scope.Resource == namespaceGVR {
156				namespace = name
157			}
158			ctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)
159	
160			admit = admission.WithAudit(admit)
161			audit.LogRequestObject(req.Context(), obj, objGV, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, scope.Serializer)
162	
163			userInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)
164	
165			if objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {
166				preserveObjectMetaSystemFields := false
167				if c, ok := r.(rest.SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver); ok && len(scope.Subresource) > 0 {
168					preserveObjectMetaSystemFields = c.PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate()
169				}
170				if !preserveObjectMetaSystemFields {
171					rest.WipeObjectMetaSystemFields(objectMeta)
172				}
173	
174				// ensure namespace on the object is correct, or error if a conflicting namespace was set in the object
175				if err := rest.EnsureObjectNamespaceMatchesRequestNamespace(rest.ExpectedNamespaceForResource(namespace, scope.Resource), objectMeta); err != nil {
176					scope.err(err, w, req)
177					return
178				}
179			}
180	
181			span.AddEvent("About to store object in database")
182			admissionAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(obj, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Create, options, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), userInfo)
183			requestFunc := func() (runtime.Object, error) {
184				return r.Create(
185					ctx,
186					name,
187					obj,
188					rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, admissionAttributes, scope),
189					options,
190				)
191			}
192			// Dedup owner references before updating managed fields
193			dedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, req.Context(), false)
194			result, err := finisher.FinishRequest(ctx, func() (runtime.Object, error) {
195				liveObj, err := scope.Creater.New(scope.Kind)
196				if err != nil {
197					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create new object (Create for %v): %v", scope.Kind, err)
198				}
199				obj = scope.FieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, obj, managerOrUserAgent(options.FieldManager, req.UserAgent()))
200				admit = fieldmanager.NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(admit)
201	
202				if mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok && mutatingAdmission.Handles(admission.Create) {
203					if err := mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admissionAttributes, scope); err != nil {
204						return nil, err
205					}
206				}
207				// Dedup owner references again after mutating admission happens
208				dedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, req.Context(), true)
209				result, err := requestFunc()
210				// If the object wasn't committed to storage because it's serialized size was too large,
211				// it is safe to remove managedFields (which can be large) and try again.
212				if isTooLargeError(err) {
213					if accessor, accessorErr := meta.Accessor(obj); accessorErr == nil {
214						accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)
215						result, err = requestFunc()
216					}
217				}
218				return result, err
219			})
220			if err != nil {
221				span.AddEvent("Write to database call failed", attribute.Int("len", len(body)), attribute.String("err", err.Error()))
222				scope.err(err, w, req)
223				return
224			}
225			span.AddEvent("Write to database call succeeded", attribute.Int("len", len(body)))
226	
227			code := http.StatusCreated
228			status, ok := result.(*metav1.Status)
229			if ok && status.Code == 0 {
230				status.Code = int32(code)
231			}
232	
233			span.AddEvent("About to write a response")
234			defer span.AddEvent("Writing http response done")
235			transformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, code, outputMediaType, result)
236		}
237	}

257	// manager is assumed to be already a valid value, we need to make
258	// userAgent into a valid value too.
259	func managerOrUserAgent(manager, userAgent string) string {
260		if manager != "" {
261			return manager
262		}
263		return prefixFromUserAgent(userAgent)
264	}
265	
266	// prefixFromUserAgent takes the characters preceding the first /, quote
267	// unprintable character and then trim what's beyond the
268	// FieldManagerMaxLength limit.
269	func prefixFromUserAgent(u string) string {
270		m := strings.Split(u, "/")[0]
271		buf := bytes.NewBuffer(nil)
272		for _, r := range m {
273			// Ignore non-printable characters
274			if !unicode.IsPrint(r) {
275				continue
276			}
277			// Only append if we have room for it
278			if buf.Len()+utf8.RuneLen(r) > validation.FieldManagerMaxLength {
279				break
280			}
281			buf.WriteRune(r)
282		}
283		return buf.String()
284	}

53	// Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.

55	// that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.
56	//

59		// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.
60		// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
61		New() runtime.Object
62	
63		// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.
64		// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared
65		// for being called more than once.
66		Destroy()
67	}

38	// RESTCreateStrategy defines the minimum validation, accepted input, and
39	// name generation behavior to create an object that follows Kubernetes
40	// API conventions.
41	type RESTCreateStrategy interface {
42		runtime.ObjectTyper
43		// The name generator is used when the standard GenerateName field is set.
44		// The NameGenerator will be invoked prior to validation.
45		names.NameGenerator
46	
47		// NamespaceScoped returns true if the object must be within a namespace.
48		NamespaceScoped() bool
49		// PrepareForCreate is invoked on create before validation to normalize
50		// the object.  For example: remove fields that are not to be persisted,
51		// sort order-insensitive list fields, etc.  This should not remove fields
52		// whose presence would be considered a validation error.
53		//
54		// Often implemented as a type check and an initailization or clearing of
55		// status. Clear the status because status changes are internal. External
56		// callers of an api (users) should not be setting an initial status on
57		// newly created objects.
58		PrepareForCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object)
59		// Validate returns an ErrorList with validation errors or nil.  Validate
60		// is invoked after default fields in the object have been filled in
61		// before the object is persisted.  This method should not mutate the
62		// object.
63		Validate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) field.ErrorList
64		// WarningsOnCreate returns warnings to the client performing a create.
65		// WarningsOnCreate is invoked after default fields in the object have been filled in
66		// and after Validate has passed, before Canonicalize is called, and the object is persisted.
67		// This method must not mutate the object.
68		//
69		// Be brief; limit warnings to 120 characters if possible.
70		// Don't include a "Warning:" prefix in the message (that is added by clients on output).
71		// Warnings returned about a specific field should be formatted as "path.to.field: message".
72		// For example: `spec.imagePullSecrets[0].name: invalid empty name ""`
73		//
74		// Use warning messages to describe problems the client making the API request should correct or be aware of.
75		// For example:
76		// - use of deprecated fields/labels/annotations that will stop working in a future release
77		// - use of obsolete fields/labels/annotations that are non-functional
78		// - malformed or invalid specifications that prevent successful handling of the submitted object,
79		//   but are not rejected by validation for compatibility reasons
80		//
81		// Warnings should not be returned for fields which cannot be resolved by the caller.
82		// For example, do not warn about spec fields in a subresource creation request.
83		WarningsOnCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) []string
84		// Canonicalize allows an object to be mutated into a canonical form. This
85		// ensures that code that operates on these objects can rely on the common
86		// form for things like comparison.  Canonicalize is invoked after
87		// validation has succeeded but before the object has been persisted.
88		// This method may mutate the object. Often implemented as a type check or
89		// empty method.
90		Canonicalize(obj runtime.Object)
91	}

93	func validatePathSegment(name string, prefix bool) []string {
94		if prefix {
95			return content.IsPathSegmentPrefix(name)
96		}
97		return content.IsPathSegmentName(name)
98	}

100	// BeforeCreate ensures that common operations for all resources are performed on creation. It only returns
101	// errors that can be converted to api.Status. It invokes PrepareForCreate, then Validate.
102	// It returns nil if the object should be created.
103	func BeforeCreate(strategy RESTCreateStrategy, ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {
104		objectMeta, kind, kerr := objectMetaAndKind(strategy, obj)
105		if kerr != nil {
106			return kerr
107		}
108	
109		// ensure that system-critical metadata has been populated
110		if !metav1.HasObjectMetaSystemFieldValues(objectMeta) {
111			return errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf("system metadata was not initialized"))
112		}
113	
114		// ensure the name has been generated
115		if len(objectMeta.GetGenerateName()) > 0 && len(objectMeta.GetName()) == 0 {
116			return errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf("metadata.name was not generated"))
117		}
118	
119		// ensure namespace on the object is correct, or error if a conflicting namespace was set in the object
120		requestNamespace, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)
121		if !ok {
122			return errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf("no namespace information found in request context"))
123		}
124		if err := EnsureObjectNamespaceMatchesRequestNamespace(ExpectedNamespaceForScope(requestNamespace, strategy.NamespaceScoped()), objectMeta); err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127	
128		strategy.PrepareForCreate(ctx, obj)
129	
130		errs := strategy.Validate(ctx, obj)
131		if dv, ok := strategy.(DeclarativeValidationStrategy); ok {
132			errs = dv.ValidateDeclaratively(ctx, obj, nil, errs, operation.Create, dv.DeclarativeValidationConfig(ctx, obj, nil))
133		}
134		if len(errs) > 0 {
135			return errors.NewInvalid(kind.GroupKind(), objectMeta.GetName(), errs)
136		}
137	
138		// Custom validation (including name validation) passed
139		// Now run common validation on object meta
140		// Do this *after* custom validation so that specific error messages are shown whenever possible
141		if errs := genericvalidation.ValidateObjectMetaAccessor(objectMeta, strategy.NamespaceScoped(), validatePathSegment, field.NewPath("metadata")); len(errs) > 0 {
142			return errors.NewInvalid(kind.GroupKind(), objectMeta.GetName(), errs)
143		}
144	
145		for _, w := range strategy.WarningsOnCreate(ctx, obj) {
146			warning.AddWarning(ctx, "", w)
147		}
148	
149		strategy.Canonicalize(obj)
150	
151		return nil
152	}

154	// CheckGeneratedNameError checks whether an error that occurred creating a resource is due
155	// to generation being unable to pick a valid name.
156	func CheckGeneratedNameError(ctx context.Context, strategy RESTCreateStrategy, err error, obj runtime.Object) error {
157		if !errors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {
158			return err
159		}
160	
161		objectMeta, gvk, kerr := objectMetaAndKind(strategy, obj)
162		if kerr != nil {
163			return kerr
164		}
165	
166		if len(objectMeta.GetGenerateName()) == 0 {
167			// If we don't have a generated name, return the original error (AlreadyExists).
168			// When we're here, the user picked a name that is causing a conflict.
169			return err
170		}
171	
172		// Get the group resource information from the context, if populated.
173		gr := schema.GroupResource{}
174		if requestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx); found {
175			gr = schema.GroupResource{Group: gvk.Group, Resource: requestInfo.Resource}
176		}
177	
178		// If we have a name and generated name, the server picked a name
179		// that already exists.
180		return errors.NewGenerateNameConflict(gr, objectMeta.GetName(), 1)
181	}

183	// objectMetaAndKind retrieves kind and ObjectMeta from a runtime object, or returns an error.
184	func objectMetaAndKind(typer runtime.ObjectTyper, obj runtime.Object) (metav1.Object, schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {
185		objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)
186		if err != nil {
187			return nil, schema.GroupVersionKind{}, errors.NewInternalError(err)
188		}
189		kinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(obj)
190		if err != nil {
191			return nil, schema.GroupVersionKind{}, errors.NewInternalError(err)
192		}
193		return objectMeta, kinds[0], nil
194	}

202	// AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc converts validating admission to a rest validate object func
203	func AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit admission.Interface, staticAttributes admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) ValidateObjectFunc {
204		validatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.ValidationInterface)
205		if !ok {
206			return func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error { return nil }
207		}
208		return func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {
209			name := staticAttributes.GetName()
210			// in case the generated name is populated
211			if len(name) == 0 {
212				if metadata, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {
213					name = metadata.GetName()
214				}
215			}
216	
217			finalAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(
218				obj,
219				staticAttributes.GetOldObject(),
220				staticAttributes.GetKind(),
221				staticAttributes.GetNamespace(),
222				name,
223				staticAttributes.GetResource(),
224				staticAttributes.GetSubresource(),
225				staticAttributes.GetOperation(),
226				staticAttributes.GetOperationOptions(),
227				staticAttributes.IsDryRun(),
228				staticAttributes.GetUserInfo(),
229			)
230			if !validatingAdmission.Handles(finalAttributes.GetOperation()) {
231				return nil
232			}
233			return validatingAdmission.Validate(ctx, finalAttributes, o)
234		}
235	}

118		// If there's a custom acceptor, delegate to it. This is extremely rare.
119		if scope.AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate != nil {

17	package admission
18	
19	import (
20		"time"
21	
22		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
23	)
24	
25	const (
26		// timeToWaitForReady is the amount of time to wait to let an admission controller to be ready to satisfy a request.
27		// this is useful when admission controllers need to warm their caches before letting requests through.
28		timeToWaitForReady = 10 * time.Second
29	)
30	
31	// ReadyFunc is a function that returns true if the admission controller is ready to handle requests.
32	type ReadyFunc func() bool
33	
34	// Handler is a base for admission control handlers that
35	// support a predefined set of operations
36	type Handler struct {
37		operations sets.Set[string]
38		readyFunc  ReadyFunc
39	}
40	
41	// Handles returns true for methods that this handler supports
42	func (h *Handler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {
43		return h.operations.Has(string(operation))
44	}
45	
46	// NewHandler creates a new base handler that handles the passed
47	// in operations
48	func NewHandler(ops ...Operation) *Handler {
49		operations := sets.New[string]()
50		for _, op := range ops {
51			operations.Insert(string(op))
52		}
53		return &Handler{
54			operations: operations,
55		}
56	}
57	
58	// SetReadyFunc allows late registration of a ReadyFunc to know if the handler is ready to process requests.
59	func (h *Handler) SetReadyFunc(readyFunc ReadyFunc) {
60		h.readyFunc = readyFunc
61	}
62	
63	// WaitForReady will wait for the readyFunc (if registered) to return ready, and in case of timeout, will return false.
64	func (h *Handler) WaitForReady() bool {
65		// there is no ready func configured, so we return immediately
66		if h.readyFunc == nil {
67			return true
68		}
69	
70		timeout := time.After(timeToWaitForReady)
71		for !h.readyFunc() {
72			select {
73			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
74			case <-timeout:
75				return h.readyFunc()
76			}
77		}
78		return true
79	}

113	// BuildGenericConfig takes the generic controlplane apiserver options and produces
114	// the genericapiserver.Config associated with it. The genericapiserver.Config is
115	// often shared between multiple delegated apiservers.
116	func BuildGenericConfig(
117		s options.CompletedOptions,
118		schemes []*runtime.Scheme,
119		resourceConfig *serverstorage.ResourceConfig,
120		getOpenAPIDefinitions func(ref openapicommon.ReferenceCallback) map[string]openapicommon.OpenAPIDefinition,
121	) (
122		genericConfig *genericapiserver.Config,
123		versionedInformers clientgoinformers.SharedInformerFactory,
124		storageFactory *serverstorage.DefaultStorageFactory,
125		lastErr error,
126	) {
127		genericConfig = genericapiserver.NewConfig(legacyscheme.Codecs)
128		genericConfig.Flagz = s.Flagz
129		genericConfig.MergedResourceConfig = resourceConfig
130	
131		if lastErr = s.GenericServerRunOptions.ApplyTo(genericConfig); lastErr != nil {
132			return
133		}
134	
135		if lastErr = s.SecureServing.ApplyToConfig(genericConfig); lastErr != nil {
136			return
137		}
138	
139		// Use protobufs for self-communication.
140		// Since not every generic apiserver has to support protobufs, we
141		// cannot default to it in generic apiserver and need to explicitly
142		// set it in kube-apiserver.
143		genericConfig.LoopbackClientConfig.ContentConfig.ContentType = "application/vnd.kubernetes.protobuf"
144		// Disable compression for self-communication, since we are going to be
145		// on a fast local network
146		genericConfig.LoopbackClientConfig.DisableCompression = true
147	
148		kubeClientConfig := genericConfig.LoopbackClientConfig
149		clientgoExternalClient, err := clientgoclientset.NewForConfig(kubeClientConfig)
150		if err != nil {
151			lastErr = fmt.Errorf("failed to create real external clientset: %w", err)
152			return
153		}
154		trim := func(obj interface{}) (interface{}, error) {
155			if accessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil && accessor.GetManagedFields() != nil {
156				accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)
157			}
158			return obj, nil
159		}
160		versionedInformers = clientgoinformers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(clientgoExternalClient, 10*time.Minute, clientgoinformers.WithTransform(trim))
161	
162		if lastErr = s.Features.ApplyTo(genericConfig, clientgoExternalClient, versionedInformers); lastErr != nil {
163			return
164		}
165		if lastErr = s.APIEnablement.ApplyTo(genericConfig, resourceConfig, legacyscheme.Scheme); lastErr != nil {
166			return
167		}
168		if lastErr = s.EgressSelector.ApplyTo(genericConfig); lastErr != nil {
169			return
170		}
171		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {
172			if lastErr = s.Traces.ApplyTo(genericConfig.EgressSelector, genericConfig); lastErr != nil {
173				return
174			}
175		}
176		// wrap the definitions to revert any changes from disabled features
177		getOpenAPIDefinitions = openapi.GetOpenAPIDefinitionsWithoutDisabledFeatures(getOpenAPIDefinitions)
178		namer := openapinamer.NewDefinitionNamer(schemes...)
179		genericConfig.OpenAPIConfig = genericapiserver.DefaultOpenAPIConfig(getOpenAPIDefinitions, namer)
180		genericConfig.OpenAPIConfig.Info.Title = "Kubernetes"
181		genericConfig.OpenAPIV3Config = genericapiserver.DefaultOpenAPIV3Config(getOpenAPIDefinitions, namer)
182		genericConfig.OpenAPIV3Config.Info.Title = "Kubernetes"
183	
184		genericConfig.LongRunningFunc = filters.BasicLongRunningRequestCheck(
185			sets.NewString("watch", "proxy"),
186			sets.NewString("attach", "exec", "proxy", "log", "portforward"),
187		)
188	
189		if genericConfig.EgressSelector != nil {
190			s.Etcd.StorageConfig.Transport.EgressLookup = genericConfig.EgressSelector.Lookup
191		}
192		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {
193			s.Etcd.StorageConfig.Transport.TracerProvider = genericConfig.TracerProvider
194		} else {
195			s.Etcd.StorageConfig.Transport.TracerProvider = noopoteltrace.NewTracerProvider()
196		}
197	
198		storageFactoryConfig := kubeapiserver.NewStorageFactoryConfigEffectiveVersion(genericConfig.EffectiveVersion)
199		storageFactoryConfig.APIResourceConfig = genericConfig.MergedResourceConfig
200		storageFactoryConfig.DefaultResourceEncoding.SetEffectiveVersion(genericConfig.EffectiveVersion)

245	// BuildAuthorizer constructs the authorizer. If authorization is not set in s, it returns nil, nil, false, nil
246	func BuildAuthorizer(ctx context.Context, s options.CompletedOptions, egressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector, apiserverID string, versionedInformers clientgoinformers.SharedInformerFactory) (authorizer.Authorizer, authorizer.RuleResolver, bool, error) {
247		authorizationConfig, err := s.Authorization.ToAuthorizationConfig(versionedInformers)
248		if err != nil {
249			return nil, nil, false, err
250		}
251		if authorizationConfig == nil {
252			return nil, nil, false, nil
253		}
254	
255		if egressSelector != nil {
256			egressDialer, err := egressSelector.Lookup(egressselector.ControlPlane.AsNetworkContext())
257			if err != nil {
258				return nil, nil, false, err
259			}
260			authorizationConfig.CustomDial = egressDialer
261		}
262	
263		enablesRBAC := false
264		for _, a := range authorizationConfig.AuthorizationConfiguration.Authorizers {
265			if string(a.Type) == modes.ModeRBAC {
266				enablesRBAC = true
267				break
268			}
269		}
270	
271		authorizer, ruleResolver, err := authorizationConfig.New(ctx, apiserverID)
272	
273		return authorizer, ruleResolver, enablesRBAC, err
274	}

31	// RecommendedOptions contains the recommended options for running an API server.
32	// If you add something to this list, it should be in a logical grouping.
33	// Each of them can be nil to leave the feature unconfigured on ApplyTo.
34	type RecommendedOptions struct {
35		Etcd           *EtcdOptions
36		SecureServing  *SecureServingOptionsWithLoopback
37		Authentication *DelegatingAuthenticationOptions
38		Authorization  *DelegatingAuthorizationOptions
39		Audit          *AuditOptions
40		Features       *FeatureOptions
41		CoreAPI        *CoreAPIOptions
42	
43		// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.
44		FeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate
45		// ExtraAdmissionInitializers is called once after all ApplyTo from the options above, to pass the returned
46		// admission plugin initializers to Admission.ApplyTo.
47		ExtraAdmissionInitializers func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error)
48		Admission                  *AdmissionOptions
49		// API Server Egress Selector is used to control outbound traffic from the API Server
50		EgressSelector *EgressSelectorOptions
51		// Traces contains options to control distributed request tracing.
52		Traces *TracingOptions
53	}
54	
55	func NewRecommendedOptions(prefix string, codec runtime.Codec) *RecommendedOptions {
56		sso := NewSecureServingOptions()
57	
58		// We are composing recommended options for an aggregated api-server,
59		// whose client is typically a proxy multiplexing many operations ---
60		// notably including long-running ones --- into one HTTP/2 connection
61		// into this server.  So allow many concurrent operations.
62		sso.HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection = 1000
63	
64		return &RecommendedOptions{
65			Etcd:           NewEtcdOptions(storagebackend.NewDefaultConfig(prefix, codec)),
66			SecureServing:  sso.WithLoopback(),
67			Authentication: NewDelegatingAuthenticationOptions(),
68			Authorization:  NewDelegatingAuthorizationOptions(),
69			Audit:          NewAuditOptions(),
70			Features:       NewFeatureOptions(),
71			CoreAPI:        NewCoreAPIOptions(),
72			// Wired a global by default that sadly people will abuse to have different meanings in different repos.
73			// Please consider creating your own FeatureGate so you can have a consistent meaning for what a variable contains
74			// across different repos.  Future you will thank you.
75			FeatureGate:                feature.DefaultFeatureGate,
76			ExtraAdmissionInitializers: func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error) { return nil, nil },
77			Admission:                  NewAdmissionOptions(),
78			EgressSelector:             NewEgressSelectorOptions(),
79			Traces:                     NewTracingOptions(),
80		}
81	}

83	func (o *RecommendedOptions) AddFlags(fs *pflag.FlagSet) {
84		o.Etcd.AddFlags(fs)
85		o.SecureServing.AddFlags(fs)
86		o.Authentication.AddFlags(fs)
87		o.Authorization.AddFlags(fs)
88		o.Audit.AddFlags(fs)
89		o.Features.AddFlags(fs)
90		o.CoreAPI.AddFlags(fs)
91		o.Admission.AddFlags(fs)
92		o.EgressSelector.AddFlags(fs)
93		o.Traces.AddFlags(fs)
94	}
95	
96	// ApplyTo adds RecommendedOptions to the server configuration.
97	// pluginInitializers can be empty, it is only need for additional initializers.
98	func (o *RecommendedOptions) ApplyTo(config *server.RecommendedConfig) error {
99		if err := o.Etcd.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
100			return err
101		}
102		if err := o.EgressSelector.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
103			return err
104		}
105		if err := o.Traces.ApplyTo(config.Config.EgressSelector, &config.Config); err != nil {
106			return err
107		}
108		if err := o.SecureServing.ApplyToConfig(&config.Config); err != nil {
109			return err
110		}
111		if err := o.Authentication.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authentication, config.SecureServing, config.OpenAPIConfig); err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		if err := o.Authorization.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authorization); err != nil {
115			return err
116		}
117		if err := o.Audit.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
118			return err
119		}
120		if err := o.CoreAPI.ApplyTo(config); err != nil {
121			return err
122		}
123		var kubeClient kubernetes.Interface
124		var dynamicClient dynamic.Interface
125		if config.ClientConfig != nil {
126			var err error
127			kubeClient, err = kubernetes.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)
128			if err != nil {
129				return err
130			}
131			dynamicClient, err = dynamic.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)
132			if err != nil {
133				return err
134			}
135		}
136		if err := o.Features.ApplyTo(&config.Config, kubeClient, config.SharedInformerFactory); err != nil {
137			return err
138		}
139		initializers, err := o.ExtraAdmissionInitializers(config)
140		if err != nil {
141			return err
142		}
143		if err := o.Admission.ApplyTo(&config.Config, config.SharedInformerFactory, kubeClient, dynamicClient, o.FeatureGate,
144			config.EffectiveVersion,
145			initializers...); err != nil {
146			return err
147		}
148		return nil
149	}
150	
151	func (o *RecommendedOptions) Validate() []error {
152		errors := []error{}
153		errors = append(errors, o.Etcd.Validate()...)
154		errors = append(errors, o.SecureServing.Validate()...)
155		errors = append(errors, o.Authentication.Validate()...)
156		errors = append(errors, o.Authorization.Validate()...)
157		errors = append(errors, o.Audit.Validate()...)
158		errors = append(errors, o.Features.Validate()...)
159		errors = append(errors, o.CoreAPI.Validate()...)
160		errors = append(errors, o.Admission.Validate()...)
161		errors = append(errors, o.EgressSelector.Validate()...)
162		errors = append(errors, o.Traces.Validate()...)
163	
164		return errors
165	}

# CREATE Pod admission ordering: research notes

## Context

Question: when a CREATE Pod request hits the kube-apiserver, in what order do mutating and validating admission plugins run, and where is that ordering enforced in code?

This is a research/explanation task — no code changes. The plan file holds the synthesized answer with file paths and line citations.

## High-level order for CREATE Pod

1. REST handler receives the decoded object
2. **Mutating admission chain runs** — every plugin implementing `MutationInterface`, in `RecommendedPluginOrder`
3. Strategy `PrepareForCreate` runs (defaulting, dropping disabled fields)
4. Strategy `Validate` runs (schema/object validation)
5. **Validating admission chain runs** — every plugin implementing `ValidationInterface`, in the same `RecommendedPluginOrder`
6. Object is written to etcd

A single plugin (e.g. `LimitRanger`, `PodSecurity`-style policy plugins, `ResourceQuota`) may implement *either* interface or *both*. The chain is iterated twice — once for mutate, once for validate — and each pass type-asserts to pick out the relevant plugins. So both phases honor the same global ordering; only "which phase does this plugin participate in" differs.

## The two enforcement points

### A. WHERE the order is *defined* — `AllOrderedPlugins`

`pkg/kubeapiserver/options/plugins.go:73-120` — single ordered slice for the whole apiserver:

```go
var AllOrderedPlugins = []string{
    admit.PluginName,                        // AlwaysAdmit
    autoprovision.PluginName,                // NamespaceAutoProvision
    lifecycle.PluginName,                    // NamespaceLifecycle
    exists.PluginName,                       // NamespaceExists
    antiaffinity.PluginName,                 // LimitPodHardAntiAffinityTopology
    limitranger.PluginName,                  // LimitRanger
    serviceaccount.PluginName,               // ServiceAccount
    noderestriction.PluginName,              // NodeRestriction
    nodetaint.PluginName,                    // TaintNodesByCondition
    alwayspullimages.PluginName,             // AlwaysPullImages
    imagepolicy.PluginName,                  // ImagePolicyWebhook
    podsecurity.PluginName,                  // PodSecurity
    podnodeselector.PluginName,              // PodNodeSelector
    podpriority.PluginName,                  // Priority
    defaulttolerationseconds.PluginName,     // DefaultTolerationSeconds
    podtolerationrestriction.PluginName,     // PodTolerationRestriction
    ...
    // new admission plugins should generally be inserted above here
    // webhook, resourcequota, and deny plugins must go at the end

    mutatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName,   // MutatingAdmissionPolicy
    mutatingwebhook.PluginName,           // MutatingAdmissionWebhook
    validatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName, // ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
    validatingwebhook.PluginName,         // ValidatingAdmissionWebhook
    resourcequota.PluginName,             // ResourceQuota
    deny.PluginName,                      // AlwaysDeny
}
```

This slice is wired in at `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/admission.go:56-68`:

```go
options.RecommendedPluginOrder = AllOrderedPlugins
```

`Plugins.NewFromPlugins(pluginNames, ...)` (in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/admission.go`) instantiates the chain in this order, returning a `chainAdmissionHandler` (a slice).

### B. WHERE the order is *executed* — `chainAdmissionHandler`

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go:31-60` — same chain, two passes:

```go
// Admit (mutating phase)
func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {
    for _, handler := range admissionHandler {
        if !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) { continue }
        if mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {
            if err := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil { return err }
        }
    }
    return nil
}

// Validate (validating phase)
func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {
    for _, handler := range admissionHandler {
        if !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) { continue }
        if validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {
            if err := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o); err != nil { return err }
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

Interfaces at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go:129-144` — `MutationInterface.Admit` may mutate; `ValidationInterface.Validate` "is NOT allowed to mutate."

### C. WHERE the two phases are sequenced for a CREATE — REST handler

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go`:

- Lines 202-206: mutating chain called explicitly first, inside the request finisher
  ```go
  if mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok && mutatingAdmission.Handles(admission.Create) {
      if err := mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admissionAttributes, scope); err != nil { return nil, err }
  }
  ```
- Lines 183-191: the *validating* chain is wrapped into a `ValidateObjectFunc` and handed to `r.Create(...)` so the storage layer can call it after defaulting + schema validation:
  ```go
  return r.Create(ctx, name, obj, rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, admissionAttributes, scope), options)
  ```

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/create.go:202-235` — `AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc` extracts only handlers implementing `ValidationInterface` and returns a func that calls `validatingAdmission.Validate(...)`.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:485-518` — inside `Store.create`:
```go
if err := rest.BeforeCreate(e.CreateStrategy, ctx, obj); err != nil { return nil, err }
// at this point we have a fully formed object.  It is time to call the validators that the apiserver
// handling chain wants to enforce.
if createValidation != nil {
    if err := createValidation(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject()); err != nil { return nil, err }
}
// ... storage.Create -> etcd
```

`BeforeCreate` runs the strategy's `PrepareForCreate` + `Validate` *between* mutating admission and validating admission.

## Concretely, for CREATE Pod

Mutating pass (in `AllOrderedPlugins` order, plugins implementing `MutationInterface`):
`NamespaceLifecycle` → `LimitRanger` (defaults) → `ServiceAccount` (injects SA + token volume) → `TaintNodesByCondition` → `AlwaysPullImages` → `PodNodeSelector` → `Priority` (defaults priorityClass) → `DefaultTolerationSeconds` → `PodTolerationRestriction` → `RuntimeClass` → `PodTopologyLabels` → `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` → `MutatingAdmissionWebhook`.

Then strategy `PrepareForCreate` + `Validate`.

Validating pass (same chain, plugins implementing `ValidationInterface`):
`NamespaceLifecycle` → `LimitPodHardAntiAffinityTopology` → `LimitRanger` (enforce) → `NodeRestriction` (rejects spoofed kubelet writes) → `ImagePolicyWebhook` → `PodSecurity` → `PodNodeSelector` → `PodTolerationRestriction` → `EventRateLimit` → `ExtendedResourceToleration` → `RuntimeClass` (enforce) → `CertificateApproval`/`CertificateSigning` (not pod-relevant, skipped via `Handles`) → `DenyServiceExternalIPs` (skipped) → `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`/`MutatingAdmissionWebhook` (skipped on validate pass) → `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` → `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` → `ResourceQuota` (must be last so it sees all prior mutations) → `AlwaysDeny`.

The "webhook, resourcequota, and deny plugins must go at the end" comment at `plugins.go:111-112` is the load-bearing constraint: `ResourceQuota` must observe the *final* mutated object before counting it against the quota, and webhooks must run after all built-in mutators so they see the canonical request.

## Key files (for citation)

- `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/plugins.go:73-120` — `AllOrderedPlugins`
- `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/admission.go:56-68` — wires `RecommendedPluginOrder`

# CREATE Pod admission ordering: research notes

## Context

Question: when a CREATE Pod request hits the kube-apiserver, in what order do mutating and validating admission plugins run, and where is that ordering enforced in code?

This is a research/explanation task — no code changes. The plan file holds the synthesized answer with file paths and line citations.

## High-level order for CREATE Pod

1. REST handler receives the decoded object
2. **Mutating admission chain runs** — every plugin implementing `MutationInterface`, in `RecommendedPluginOrder`
3. Strategy `PrepareForCreate` runs (defaulting, dropping disabled fields)
4. Strategy `Validate` runs (schema/object validation)
5. **Validating admission chain runs** — every plugin implementing `ValidationInterface`, in the same `RecommendedPluginOrder`
6. Object is written to etcd

A single plugin (e.g. `LimitRanger`, `PodSecurity`-style policy plugins, `ResourceQuota`) may implement *either* interface or *both*. The chain is iterated twice — once for mutate, once for validate — and each pass type-asserts to pick out the relevant plugins. So both phases honor the same global ordering; only "which phase does this plugin participate in" differs.

## The two enforcement points

### A. WHERE the order is *defined* — `AllOrderedPlugins`

`pkg/kubeapiserver/options/plugins.go:73-120` — single ordered slice for the whole apiserver:

```go
var AllOrderedPlugins = []string{
    admit.PluginName,                        // AlwaysAdmit
    autoprovision.PluginName,                // NamespaceAutoProvision
    lifecycle.PluginName,                    // NamespaceLifecycle
    exists.PluginName,                       // NamespaceExists
    antiaffinity.PluginName,                 // LimitPodHardAntiAffinityTopology
    limitranger.PluginName,                  // LimitRanger
    serviceaccount.PluginName,               // ServiceAccount
    noderestriction.PluginName,              // NodeRestriction
    nodetaint.PluginName,                    // TaintNodesByCondition
    alwayspullimages.PluginName,             // AlwaysPullImages
    imagepolicy.PluginName,                  // ImagePolicyWebhook
    podsecurity.PluginName,                  // PodSecurity
    podnodeselector.PluginName,              // PodNodeSelector
    podpriority.PluginName,                  // Priority
    defaulttolerationseconds.PluginName,     // DefaultTolerationSeconds
    podtolerationrestriction.PluginName,     // PodTolerationRestriction
    ...
    // new admission plugins should generally be inserted above here
    // webhook, resourcequota, and deny plugins must go at the end

    mutatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName,   // MutatingAdmissionPolicy
    mutatingwebhook.PluginName,           // MutatingAdmissionWebhook
    validatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName, // ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
    validatingwebhook.PluginName,         // ValidatingAdmissionWebhook
    resourcequota.PluginName,             // ResourceQuota
    deny.PluginName,                      // AlwaysDeny
}
```

This slice is wired in at `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/admission.go:56-68`:

```go
options.RecommendedPluginOrder = AllOrderedPlugins
```

`Plugins.NewFromPlugins(pluginNames, ...)` (in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/admission.go`) instantiates the chain in this order, returning a `chainAdmissionHandler` (a slice).

### B. WHERE the order is *executed* — `chainAdmissionHandler`

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go:31-60` — same chain, two passes:

```go
// Admit (mutating phase)
func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {
    for _, handler := range admissionHandler {
        if !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) { continue }
        if mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {
            if err := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil { return err }
        }
    }
    return nil
}

// Validate (validating phase)
func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {
    for _, handler := range admissionHandler {
        if !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) { continue }
        if validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {
            if err := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o); err != nil { return err }
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

Interfaces at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go:129-144` — `MutationInterface.Admit` may mutate; `ValidationInterface.Validate` "is NOT allowed to mutate."

### C. WHERE the two phases are sequenced for a CREATE — REST handler

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go`:

- Lines 202-206: mutating chain called explicitly first, inside the request finisher
  ```go
  if mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok && mutatingAdmission.Handles(admission.Create) {
      if err := mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admissionAttributes, scope); err != nil { return nil, err }
  }
  ```
- Lines 183-191: the *validating* chain is wrapped into a `ValidateObjectFunc` and handed to `r.Create(...)` so the storage layer can call it after defaulting + schema validation:
  ```go
  return r.Create(ctx, name, obj, rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, admissionAttributes, scope), options)
  ```

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/create.go:202-235` — `AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc` extracts only handlers implementing `ValidationInterface` and returns a func that calls `validatingAdmission.Validate(...)`.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:485-518` — inside `Store.create`:
```go
if err := rest.BeforeCreate(e.CreateStrategy, ctx, obj); err != nil { return nil, err }
// at this point we have a fully formed object.  It is time to call the validators that the apiserver
// handling chain wants to enforce.
if createValidation != nil {
    if err := createValidation(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject()); err != nil { return nil, err }
}
// ... storage.Create -> etcd
```

`BeforeCreate` runs the strategy's `PrepareForCreate` + `Validate` *between* mutating admission and validating admission.

## Concretely, for CREATE Pod

Mutating pass (in `AllOrderedPlugins` order, plugins implementing `MutationInterface`):
`NamespaceLifecycle` → `LimitRanger` (defaults) → `ServiceAccount` (injects SA + token volume) → `TaintNodesByCondition` → `AlwaysPullImages` → `PodNodeSelector` → `Priority` (defaults priorityClass) → `DefaultTolerationSeconds` → `PodTolerationRestriction` → `RuntimeClass` → `PodTopologyLabels` → `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` → `MutatingAdmissionWebhook`.

Then strategy `PrepareForCreate` + `Validate`.

Validating pass (same chain, plugins implementing `ValidationInterface`):
`NamespaceLifecycle` → `LimitPodHardAntiAffinityTopology` → `LimitRanger` (enforce) → `NodeRestriction` (rejects spoofed kubelet writes) → `ImagePolicyWebhook` → `PodSecurity` → `PodNodeSelector` → `PodTolerationRestriction` → `EventRateLimit` → `ExtendedResourceToleration` → `RuntimeClass` (enforce) → `CertificateApproval`/`CertificateSigning` (not pod-relevant, skipped via `Handles`) → `DenyServiceExternalIPs` (skipped) → `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`/`MutatingAdmissionWebhook` (skipped on validate pass) → `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` → `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` → `ResourceQuota` (must be last so it sees all prior mutations) → `AlwaysDeny`.

The "webhook, resourcequota, and deny plugins must go at the end" comment at `plugins.go:111-112` is the load-bearing constraint: `ResourceQuota` must observe the *final* mutated object before counting it against the quota, and webhooks must run after all built-in mutators so they see the canonical request.

## Key files (for citation)

- `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/admission.go:56-68` — wires `RecommendedPluginOrder`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go:31-60` — `Admit` / `Validate` chain iteration
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go:129-144` — `MutationInterface` / `ValidationInterface`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go:183-206` — explicit mutate-then-pass-validator-down for CREATE
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/create.go:202-235` — `AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:485-518` — strategy validate, then validating admission, then etcd
